title: Beginner
description: Build real conversational ability — colors, family, food, time, transport, and shopping

units.title: Colors & Descriptions
units.description: Describe the world around you using colors and adjectives

lessons.title: Colors
lessons.description: Learn all the essential Dutch colors

exercises.translation: The sky is ___.

lessons.title: Describing Objects
lessons.description: Use colors and adjectives to describe everyday objects

exercises.question: How do you say 'the red car'?

exercises.translation: I have a red car.
The yellow house is big.
The red car is fast.

lessons.title: Opposites
lessons.description: Learn key adjective opposites used in everyday Dutch

exercises.sentence: Deze tas is erg ___, niet goedkoop.
exercises.translation: This bag is very expensive, not cheap.
The weather is warm today.

lessons.title: Describing People
lessons.description: Describe people's appearance and personality

exercises.question: How do you say 'she is friendly'?

exercises.translation: My brother is very funny.

exercises.question: What did you hear about her?

lessons.title: Describing Your Home
lessons.description: Combine colors, adjectives and nouns to describe your living space

exercises.question: How do you say 'a small bathroom'?

exercises.translation: Our living room is very cozy.

exercises.correctAnswer: Mijn slaapkamer is klein maar gezellig.
exercises.translation: My bedroom is small but cozy.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review colors, adjectives, opposites, and descriptions

exercises.translation: The black dog is kind.
He is happy, not sad.

exercises.correctAnswer: Het huis is groot en modern.
exercises.translation: The house is big and modern.

units.title: Family & People
units.description: Talk about your family and the people in your life

lessons.title: Immediate Family
lessons.description: Learn the Dutch words for your closest family members

exercises.translation: My father is called Jan.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik heb een broer en een zus.
exercises.translation: I have a brother and a sister.

lessons.title: Extended Family
lessons.description: Learn the Dutch words for your extended family members

exercises.translation: My grandfather lives in Rotterdam.

lessons.title: Relationships
lessons.description: Talk about the people in your life and your relationships

exercises.question: What does 'vriend' mean in context 'Dit is mijn vriend Tom'?
options: This is my female friend Tom

exercises.question: How do you say 'my female neighbor'?

exercises.translation: This is my girlfriend Sara.
My colleague is very kind.

lessons.title: Possessives
lessons.description: Use possessive pronouns correctly in Dutch

exercises.question: How do you say 'her house is big'?
Which possessive is used before a 'het' word when meaning 'our'?

exercises.translation: Our house is big.
What is your father called?

lessons.title: Talking About Family
lessons.description: Use natural sentences to talk about your family

exercises.question: How do you say 'I am an only child'?

exercises.sentence: Ik ben ___, ik heb geen man of vrouw.
exercises.translation: I am single, I have no husband or wife.
Where do your parents live?

exercises.correctAnswer: Mijn moeder is lerares in Amsterdam.
exercises.translation: My mother is a teacher in Amsterdam.

exercises.dutch: Ik heb twee broers en één zus.
exercises.question: What did you hear?
options: I have one brother and two sisters.
I have two brothers and one sister.
I have two sisters and one brother.
exercises.correctAnswer: I have two brothers and one sister.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review family vocabulary, relationships, and possessives

exercises.english: I am married and have two children.
exercises.correctAnswer: Ik ben getrouwd en heb twee kinderen.

exercises.translation: This is my brother.

exercises.sentence: Mijn ___ woont in een klein dorp.
exercises.translation: My grandmother lives in a small village.
Are you married or single?

units.title: Food & Drink
units.description: Order food and drink and talk about what you like to eat

lessons.title: Basic Food
lessons.description: Learn essential Dutch food vocabulary

exercises.translation: I eat bread every day.

lessons.title: Drinks
lessons.description: Learn Dutch vocabulary for all kinds of drinks

exercises.question: How do you say 'a glass of water'?

exercises.translation: I like to drink coffee in the morning.

exercises.dutch: een fles water en een glas wijn
exercises.question: What did you hear?
options: een glas water en een fles bier
een kopje koffie en een glas thee
een fles water en een glas wijn
een glas sap en een fles melk
exercises.correctAnswer: een fles water en een glas wijn

lessons.title: At the Café
lessons.description: Order food and drinks at a Dutch café

exercises.question: How do you politely ask for a coffee?

exercises.dutch: Ik wil graag een kopje thee.
exercises.correctAnswer: I would like a cup of tea.
acceptedAnswers: I would like a cup of tea.
I'd like a cup of tea.

exercises.english: Do you have a table for two?
exercises.correctAnswer: Heeft u een tafel voor twee?

exercises.sentence: Ik wil graag een koffie ___ melk.
exercises.translation: I would like a coffee with milk.
Can I have the bill, please?

exercises.dutch: Ik wil graag een kopje koffie met melk.
exercises.question: What did the person order?
options: a cup of tea with sugar
a cup of coffee with milk
a glass of juice with ice
a cup of coffee with sugar
exercises.correctAnswer: a cup of coffee with milk

lessons.title: Likes & Dislikes
lessons.description: Express what food you love and what you can't stand

exercises.question: What is the best translation of 'Ik lust geen vis'?
options: I don't like to eat fish.
exercises.correctAnswer: I don't like to eat fish.

exercises.english: What do you like to eat most?

exercises.translation: Chocolate is very tasty!
I don't like to eat meat.

exercises.dutch: Ik hou van koffie maar ik lust geen thee.
exercises.question: What did you hear?
options: I love tea but I don't like coffee.
I love coffee but I don't like to drink tea.
I like milk but not coffee.
exercises.correctAnswer: I love coffee but I don't like to drink tea.

lessons.title: Eating Verbs & Meals
lessons.description: Talk about meals and use eating verbs correctly

exercises.question: Which conjugation is correct for 'jij' with the verb 'drinken'?

exercises.translation: What do you eat in the morning?

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik eet elke dag brood 's ochtends.
exercises.translation: I eat bread every morning.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review food, drinks, café ordering, and eating verbs

exercises.question: How do you ask for the bill formally?
What is the difference between 'ik lust' and 'ik hou van'?
options: 'Ik lust' is specifically for food; 'ik hou van' is more general love/liking.
'Ik hou van' is only for people.
'Ik lust' is formal and 'ik hou van' is informal.
exercises.correctAnswer: 'Ik lust' is specifically for food; 'ik hou van' is more general love/liking.
a cup of tea with sugar

exercises.english: I would like a bottle of water.
exercises.correctAnswer: Ik wil graag een fles water.

exercises.translation: Do you have a table for two?
I drink coffee every morning.
What do you like to eat most?

exercises.dutch: Mag ik een koffie met melk en een stuk taart?
exercises.question: What did the person order?
options: a tea with sugar and a sandwich
a coffee with milk and a piece of cake
a coffee with sugar and a cookie
a juice and a piece of bread
exercises.correctAnswer: a coffee with milk and a piece of cake

units.title: Days, Months & Time
units.description: Talk about days of the week, months, and tell the time in Dutch

lessons.title: Days of the Week
lessons.description: Learn the days of the week and talk about your weekly schedule

exercises.translation: On ___ I go to school.
On Monday I work.

exercises.question: What is being asked?
options: What do you do on Friday?
What do you do on Monday?
When do you work on Friday?
exercises.correctAnswer: What do you do on Friday?

lessons.title: Months of the Year
lessons.description: Learn all twelve months and talk about seasons and birthdays

exercises.question: What does 'Wanneer is je verjaardag?' mean?

exercises.translation: My birthday is in March.
In January it is cold.

lessons.title: Telling the Time
lessons.description: Ask and tell the time in Dutch, including half hours and quarter hours

exercises.question: What does 'Hoe laat is het?' mean?
How do you say 4:45 in Dutch?

exercises.translation: It is half past two. (2:30)

lessons.title: Daily Time Expressions
lessons.description: Express frequency and parts of the day in Dutch

exercises.translation: I always go to the gym.
I sometimes read in the evening.

lessons.title: Talking About Schedules
lessons.description: Discuss schedules, opening hours, and appointments in Dutch

exercises.question: What does 'Hoe lang duurt het?' mean?
options: How long have you been here?

exercises.question: What does 'Ik heb een afspraak.' mean?
How do you say 'The shop is open from nine to five'?
options: De winkel is open van negen tot vijf.
De winkel is open om negen uur.
De winkel gaat open tot vijf.
De winkel is van negen open vijf.
exercises.correctAnswer: De winkel is open van negen tot vijf.

exercises.dutch: De trein vertrekt om acht uur.
exercises.correctAnswer: The train departs at eight o'clock.
acceptedAnswers: The train departs at eight o'clock.
The train leaves at eight o'clock.

exercises.sentence: De winkel is open ___ negen tot vijf.
exercises.translation: The shop is open from nine to five.

exercises.dutch: Ik heb een afspraak om drie uur.
exercises.question: What does the speaker have at three o'clock?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review days, months, telling the time, and schedule vocabulary

exercises.english: It is never cold in July.
exercises.correctAnswer: Het is nooit koud in juli.

exercises.translation: The train departs at eight o'clock.
On Friday I am at home.

exercises.question: What does 'Hoe laat is het?' mean?

units.title: Getting Around
units.description: Navigate Dutch cities using public transport and asking for directions

lessons.title: Transport Types
lessons.description: Learn the vocabulary for different modes of transport in the Netherlands

exercises.question: How do you say 'by train' in Dutch?

exercises.sentence: Ik ga ___ de fiets naar mijn werk.
exercises.translation: I go to work by bike.

exercises.dutch: Ik ga met de trein naar Amsterdam.
exercises.question: How does the speaker travel to Amsterdam?

lessons.title: Directions
lessons.description: Learn directional vocabulary to navigate Dutch streets

exercises.question: How do you say 'the intersection' in Dutch?

exercises.translation: The bank is opposite the post office.
Take the first street on the left.

lessons.title: Asking for Directions
lessons.description: Ask and give directions in Dutch using polite phrases

exercises.question: What does 'Pardon, waar is het station?' mean?
options: Excuse me, how far is the station?
Excuse me, where is the station?
Excuse me, is this the station?
Excuse me, when does the station open?
exercises.correctAnswer: Excuse me, where is the station?

exercises.question: What does 'Hoe kom ik bij het museum?' mean?
options: How do I get to the museum?
How long does the museum take?
exercises.correctAnswer: How do I get to the museum?

exercises.question: Someone says 'U moet rechtsaf.' What should you do?

exercises.translation: Excuse me, where is the supermarket?

exercises.dutch: Gaat u rechtdoor en dan rechtsaf.
exercises.question: What are the directions given?
options: Go straight ahead and then turn right.
Go straight ahead and then turn left.
exercises.correctAnswer: Go straight ahead and then turn right.

lessons.title: Public Transport
lessons.description: Navigate the Dutch public transport system with confidence

exercises.sentence: Vergeet niet te ___ als je de bus uitstapt.
exercises.translation: Don't forget to check out when you exit the bus.

exercises.dutch: Er is een vertraging van tien minuten.
exercises.question: What is happening?
options: The train is ten minutes early.
There is a ten minute delay.
exercises.correctAnswer: There is a ten minute delay.

lessons.title: Buying Tickets
lessons.description: Buy train tickets and get travel information in Dutch

exercises.question: What does 'Enkele reis of retour?' mean?
How do you ask 'When does the next train leave?'
options: Hoe laat vertrekt de volgende trein?
Hoe lang duurt de volgende trein?
Waar gaat de volgende trein naartoe?
exercises.correctAnswer: Hoe laat vertrekt de volgende trein?

exercises.translation: I want a ticket to Utrecht.

exercises.question: What is the ticket agent asking?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review transport types, directions, and public transport vocabulary

exercises.question: What is the most popular form of transport in the Netherlands?
How do you say 'Excuse me, where is the station?' in Dutch?
options: Hoe kom ik bij het station?

exercises.english: A single ticket to Rotterdam, please.
exercises.correctAnswer: Een enkele reis naar Rotterdam, alsjeblieft.
acceptedAnswers: Een enkele reis naar Rotterdam, alsjeblieft.
Een enkele reis naar Rotterdam, alstublieft.

exercises.translation: You need to turn left at the intersection.
What time does the train depart?

exercises.dutch: De trein vertrekt op spoor vijf.
exercises.question: Where does the train depart from?

exercises.sentence: Ik ga met de bus. Ik moet ___ inchecken.
exercises.translation: I'm taking the bus. I also need to check in.

units.title: Shopping & Money
units.description: Shop confidently in Dutch — supermarkets, clothing stores, and more

lessons.title: Numbers & Prices
lessons.description: Learn larger numbers and how to talk about prices in Dutch

exercises.question: How do you say '21' in Dutch?

exercises.translation: That costs forty euros.

lessons.title: At the Supermarket
lessons.description: Learn vocabulary for shopping at a Dutch supermarket

exercises.question: What does 'boodschappen doen' mean?
options: to do the shopping / buy groceries
exercises.correctAnswer: to do the shopping / buy groceries

exercises.translation: Do you need a bag?

exercises.dutch: Ik doe mijn boodschappen in de supermarkt.
exercises.question: Where does the speaker do their shopping?

lessons.title: Clothing
lessons.description: Learn clothing vocabulary and how to shop for clothes in Dutch

exercises.question: What does 'Mag ik dit passen?' mean?

exercises.translation: What is your size?
Can I try this on?

lessons.title: Shopping Phrases
lessons.description: Learn essential phrases for interacting with shop assistants in Dutch

exercises.question: What does 'Ik zoek een trui.' mean?
options: I want to buy a sweater.

exercises.question: How do you say 'I'll take it.'?

exercises.english: I'll leave it. / I won't take it.

exercises.translation: Do you have this in size M?

exercises.dutch: Ik zoek een jas, maar deze is te duur.
exercises.question: What is the problem?
options: The jacket is the wrong colour.

lessons.title: Paying
lessons.description: Learn how to handle payments and returns in Dutch shops

exercises.question: What does 'Ik wil dit terugbrengen.' mean?
options: I want a receipt for this.
I want to pay for this.

exercises.question: How do you ask 'Do you have anything smaller?' (asking for change)?

exercises.translation: Are you paying cash or by debit card?

exercises.dutch: Ik wil dit terugbrengen. Mag ik de bon zien?
exercises.question: What does the shop assistant want to see?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review numbers, supermarket, clothing, shopping phrases, and paying vocabulary

exercises.sentence: Ik neem het ___. Het is te klein.
exercises.translation: I won't take it. It's too small.

exercises.correctAnswer: Heeft u dit in maat M?
exercises.translation: Do you have this in size M?
Are you paying cash or by debit card?

units.title: Hobbies & Free Time
units.description: Talk about what you enjoy doing in your free time

lessons.title: Sports & Activities
lessons.description: Vocabulary for common sports and physical activities

exercises.translation: I run five kilometres every morning.
He plays football every week.

lessons.title: Music & Arts
lessons.description: Talking about music and artistic hobbies

exercises.translation: She plays piano very well.
I love classical music.

lessons.title: Reading & Films
lessons.description: Talking about books, films, and series

exercises.translation: I like reading thrillers.
Which film do you want to see?

lessons.title: Social Activities
lessons.description: Activities you do with others

exercises.sentence: Zullen we vanavond ___ in de stad?
exercises.translation: Shall we meet up in town tonight?

exercises.correctAnswer: We gaan zaterdag naar een concert.
exercises.translation: We are going to a concert on Saturday.

lessons.title: Talking About Hobbies
lessons.description: Expressing what you like and dislike doing

exercises.question: How do you say 'I like reading' in Dutch?

acceptedAnswers: Ik doe niet graag aan sport.

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ van wandelen in de natuur.
exercises.translation: I love walking in nature.

exercises.correctAnswer: Mijn hobby is koken en bakken.
exercises.translation: My hobby is cooking and baking.

exercises.question: What does 'Dat is niet mijn ding' mean?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 7 content

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ elke week naar de sportschool.
exercises.translation: I go to the gym every week.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ze speelt graag gitaar in haar vrije tijd.
exercises.translation: She likes playing guitar in her free time.

exercises.question: What does 'Ik hou van lezen' mean?

exercises.translation: My favourite hobby is walking.

units.title: Feelings & Emotions
units.description: Express how you feel and describe emotions in Dutch

lessons.title: Basic Feelings
lessons.description: Essential Dutch words for how you feel

exercises.translation: I feel happy today.
How do you feel?

lessons.title: Expressing Happiness & Sadness
lessons.description: Talk about joy and sadness in Dutch

exercises.question: What does 'Ik ben zo blij!' mean?

exercises.sentence: Ze ___ omdat ze zo blij is.
exercises.translation: She is laughing because she is so happy.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik ben zo blij met dit nieuws.
exercises.translation: I am so happy with this news.

lessons.title: Anger & Frustration
lessons.description: Expressing anger and frustration in Dutch

exercises.translation: He is angry with his colleague.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik ben zo gefrustreerd door dit probleem.
exercises.translation: I am so frustrated by this problem.

lessons.title: Love & Relationships
lessons.description: Vocabulary for love, friendship, and relationships

exercises.translation: I am in love with you.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ze zijn al tien jaar getrouwd.
exercises.translation: They have been married for ten years.

lessons.title: Describing Personality
lessons.description: Words to describe people's character

exercises.sentence: Mijn vriend is heel ___ en behulpzaam.
exercises.translation: My friend is very kind and helpful.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ze is erg grappig en heeft een goed gevoel voor humor.
exercises.translation: She is very funny and has a good sense of humour.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 8 content

exercises.sentence: Ik voel me ___ omdat ik slecht heb geslapen.
exercises.translation: I feel tired because I slept badly.
How do you feel today?

exercises.sentence: Ze is heel ___ en helpt altijd anderen.
exercises.translation: She is very kind and always helps others.
